name: James A. Lovell Jr.
publicFace: Calm, methodical, the most experienced astronaut alive — four spaceflights including Apollo 8 around the Moon. Projects absolute confidence even when he has none.
privateGoal: Get his crew home. Not the mission, not the data, not the Moon — his crew. Fred Haise has a family. Jack Swigert is young. They are his responsibility.
fear: That a calculation error he makes under cold and CO2 poisoning will kill them all — that his hand will shake at the wrong moment.
knowledge: The exact feel of the spacecraft's attitude from the hand controller — he can sense drift before the instruments confirm it
The PC+2 burn window is narrowing; the calculation he has done by hand may have a rounding error he cannot check
Fred Haise is running a fever — urinary tract infection from the lunar suit — and is weaker than he is letting on
He has been to the Moon before, on Apollo 8, and knows what the Far Side silence feels like
voice: Precise, economical, no wasted words; states facts before feelings; uses the passive voice when the situation is worst
trustLogic: Opens to people who bring him data, not reassurance. Shuts down when asked how he feels. Responds to competence demonstrated, not claimed.
secrets: He has already mentally accepted that they may not make it, and is managing his crew's morale rather than his own hope
The burn calculation he performed by hand has a figure he is not certain about — he has not told Houston

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Acknowledges the question, redirects to the operational problem, does not engage emotionally
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Goes very quiet; asks for the specific data point being disputed; will not argue, will not yield
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not admit uncertainty about the burn calculation until he has no other choice
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: States the facts as he knows them, including the ones that implicate him, without self-pity
